in.d: a word that shows something is inside or part of something else
in.e: The cat is sleeping in the box.

is.d: a word that shows something exists or is true

it.d: a thing or animal we are talking about
it.e: The dog chased the cat, but it got away.

if.e: If it rains, we can't go outside.

its.d: Its shows that something belongs to or is connected to something else.
its.e: The dog is wagging its tail.

into.d: Into means moving or going towards the inside of something.
into.e: The cat jumped into the box.

including.d: to have as a part of the whole
including.e: My lunch including an apple and a sandwich.

important.e: Washing your hands is an important way to stay healthy.

international.e: The Olympics is an international sporting event.

information.e: The book has a lot of information about animals.

idea.d: A thought or plan in your mind
idea.e: I had an idea to make a new game.

interest.d: A topic or activity that you enjoy and want to learn more about
interest.e: My main interest is learning about outer space and the planets.

include.d: To put something in or make it part of something else
include.e: We include our favorite toys in our backpack for school.

issue.d: A problem or difficulty that needs to be solved
issue.e: The broken window is an issue we need to fix.

instead.e: I wanted ice cream, but I had fruit instead.

issues.d: Problems or concerns that need to be dealt with
issues.e: The club is working to address the issues in our community.

inside.d: The area or space within something
inside.e: I found a dollar bill inside my pocket.

industry.d: The work and businesses of making or selling things.
industry.e: The factory was an important part of the local industry.

involved.d: When you take part in something or help with it.
involved.e: The teacher got the students involved in the school play.

itself.d: The thing itself, not anything else.
itself.e: The cat curled up by itself on the couch.

internet.d: A global computer network that allows people to communicate
internet.e: I use the internet at home to play games and do my homework.

ii.d: The number after one and before three

increase.d: To make something bigger or more
increase.e: I will increase the size of my drawing.

included.d: To be a part of something
included.e: The cookies were included in the box of treats.

individual.e: Each individual student in the class got a prize.

island.e: The family went on a boat trip to the tropical island.

ice.d: The solid, frozen form of water
ice.e: I put ice cubes in my cold drink on a hot day.

income.d: The money a person or family earns
income.e: My parents' income helps pay for our house and food.

image.d: An image is a picture or drawing of something.
image.e: The photograph is a nice image of my family on vacation.

immediately.d: Immediately means right away, without any delay.
immediately.e: I need to clean my room immediately before my friends come over.

independent.d: Able to do things by yourself
independent.e: The little girl is independent and dresses herself.

increased.d: When something becomes bigger or more
increased.e: The number of people in our class has increased this year.

india.d: A country in Asia with a very rich culture
india.e: My family is planning a trip to visit India next summer.

interesting.d: Something that is fun or exciting to learn about
interesting.e: That science project looks really interesting.

includes.d: To have something as a part of a whole
includes.e: The package includes a book, a pencil, and some paper.

impact.e: The new school program had a big impact on the students.

interested.d: wanting to learn more about something
interested.e: I'm interested in learning about different animals.

israel.d: a country in the Middle East
israel.e: My family visited Israel on our vacation last year.

indian.d: a person from the country of India
indian.e: My friend's family is Indian and they cook delicious food.

investment.d: When you put money into something to make more money later.
investment.e: My parents invested in a new house.

imagine.d: To picture something in your mind
imagine.e: I can imagine a dragon flying in the sky.

ideas.d: Thoughts or plans that you have in your mind
ideas.e: I had a great idea to build a tree house in the backyard.

indeed.d: Used to emphasize something is true
indeed.e: Yes, indeed, I did see a shooting star last night.

insurance.d: Something that helps protect you if something bad happens
insurance.e: My parents have insurance in case they get sick.

interview.d: A conversation where someone asks you questions
interview.e: The teacher interviewed the new student.

individuals.d: Single people or things, not a group.
individuals.e: The class has 20 individuals, each with their own desk.

influence.d: The power to change or affect something
influence.e: The teacher's kind words had a strong influence on the student.

impossible.d: Something that cannot be done or happen.
impossible.e: It's impossible to ride a bike underwater.

industrial.d: Related to factories, machines, and the making of products
industrial.e: The industrial area of the city has many factories.

institute.d: A school or organization that teaches a specific subject
institute.e: She studied art at the local art institute.

items.d: Things or objects that are part of a list or group
items.e: The store had many items for sale, like toys and clothes.

images.d: Pictures or drawings that you can see.
images.e: The book had beautiful images of animals.

interests.d: Things that you like to do or learn about.
interests.e: Her main interests are reading and playing sports.

ill.e: I stayed home from school because I was feeling ill.

initial.d: The first or starting part of something.
initial.e: The initial plan was to go to the park, but then we changed our minds.

italy.d: A country in Europe with a lot of history and culture.
italy.e: My family took a trip to Italy to see the ancient ruins.

introduced.d: To make someone or something known for the first time.
introduced.e: The teacher introduced the new student to the class.

intended.d: Something that is planned or meant to happen.
intended.e: The teacher intended to give us a surprise quiz.

internal.d: Something that is on the inside or within.
internal.e: My stomach made an internal noise when I was hungry.

iron.d: A metal that is used to make many things.
iron.e: My mom uses an iron to smooth out the wrinkles in my clothes.

italian.d: Something from the country of Italy.
italian.e: My favorite food is Italian pizza.

inc.d: A company or business that is incorporated
inc.e: The store I went to is called Inc.

increasing.d: becoming greater or more in number or amount
increasing.e: The new puppy is increasing in size every day.

intelligence.d: the ability to learn and understand things
intelligence.e: My little sister has a lot of intelligence and does well in school.

issued.d: to officially give or send out
issued.e: The teacher issued each student a new notebook.

iii.d: a way to write the number three
iii.e: My sister is the third child in our family, so her name has iii.

importance.d: the quality of being important or valuable
importance.e: Washing your hands is of great importance to stay healthy.

institutions.d: Important organizations that provide services to the public.
institutions.e: The school is an important institution in the community.

identified.d: to recognize or figure out who or what something is
identified.e: The detective identified the thief at the crime scene.

identity.d: Who a person is and what makes them unique
identity.e: The students learned about the importance of having a strong personal identity.

iran.d: A country in the Middle East
iran.e: My friend's family came to our country from Iran.

injury.d: hurt or damage to your body
injury.e: He got an injury when he fell off his bike.

illegal.d: Something that is not allowed by the law
illegal.e: It is illegal to drive a car without a license.

independence.d: The freedom to do things on your own
independence.e: When I grow up, I want to live independently from my parents.

instance.e: For instance, I like to eat pizza for dinner.

ireland.d: a country in Europe with green hills and friendly people
ireland.e: My family went on vacation to Ireland and saw castles and shamrocks.

index.d: A list that helps you find information quickly
index.e: The index at the back of the book shows all the pages.

immediate.d: Immediate means happening right away, without delay.
immediate.e: The teacher gave the students immediate feedback on their work.

improved.d: Improved means made better or more effective than before.
improved.e: The team improved their game plan for the next match.

identify.d: To recognize or figure out what something is
identify.e: I can identify the different types of flowers in the garden.

inspired.d: Feeling excited and motivated to do something.
inspired.e: The inspiring story made me want to help others.

invited.d: When someone asks you to come to an event or join them.
invited.e: I was invited to my friend's birthday party.

item.e: Each item on the list needs to be checked.

incident.d: Something that happens, often by surprise.
incident.e: The dog jumping on the table was an incident that surprised us.

introduction.d: A way to meet someone or tell them about you.
introduction.e: I gave a friendly introduction to my new classmate.

increases.e: The plant increases in size every day.

incredible.e: The magician did an incredible trick.

iraq.d: a country in the Middle East
iraq.e: Iraq is located near the Persian Gulf.

irish.d: relating to the people or culture of Ireland
irish.e: The Irish music was very lively.

islands.d: a piece of land surrounded by water
islands.e: The tropical islands had beautiful beaches.

id.d: Short for identification, like a card or number
id.e: I need to show my ID to buy tickets.

involving.d: Includes or has to do with something
involving.e: The project involving building a birdhouse.

ideal.e: My toy robot is the ideal birthday gift.

interior.e: The interior of the car has lots of cool features.

improvement.e: The new playground is a great improvement for the kids.

indicated.d: To show or point something out
indicated.e: The sign on the door indicated where to enter.

initially.d: At the beginning or start of something
initially.e: I was nervous at first, but then I started to have fun.

institution.e: The university is a well-known educational institution.

increasingly.d: When something happens more and more.
increasingly.e: The rain was falling increasingly hard.

indicate.d: To show or point out something.
indicate.e: The sign indicated where the library was.

info.e: The website had lots of useful info about the trip.

iv.d: Short for 'intravenous', a way to give medicine directly into the blood.
iv.e: The nurse gave the patient an iv in the hospital.

ignore.d: to not pay attention to something
ignore.e: I tried to ignore the loud noise outside.

infrastructure.d: the basic systems and services that a country or organization uses
infrastructure.e: The city has a good infrastructure with roads and bridges.

inner.d: the part of something that is on the inside
inner.e: She kept her secret feelings deep inside her inner thoughts.

islamic.d: related to the religion of Islam
islamic.e: The Islamic holy book is called the Quran.

israeli.d: related to the country of Israel
israeli.e: The Israeli flag has a blue and white design.
